 ### 5. Broader Decor discrepancies exist outside the single-folder example
 
 A separate local comparison report for `Generic Decor.old` vs current `Generic Decor` found:
 
 - 70 files missing by same relative path
 - 45 directories missing by same relative path
 - those 70 files were not found elsewhere in the base tree by content hash
 
 So the total problem set is broader than one `PPS Photos` folder.
+
+## Backend Problem Ledger
+
+The following ledger was pulled from the live Synology Monitor backend on June 5, 2026. It covers unique grouped `issues` updated between April 5, 2026 and June 5, 2026. Raw `alerts` and `facts` in the same window were also checked; they mainly support the same grouped issue families: mass rename/ransomware-class alerts, disk I/O pressure, ShareSync queue jams, and secure-login/socket failures.
+
+As of the query time, the backend had 17 unique issue threads in this two-month window. None were marked resolved.
+
+| ID | First seen | Last updated | NAS | Severity | Status | Problem |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| `babfc04f` | 2026-04-12 | 2026-06-03 | both | warning | waiting_on_user | ShareSync API failures in `admin_console` |
+| `f5ef174d` | 2026-04-28 | 2026-05-31 | `edgesynology1` | critical | open | Mass rename activity |
+| `7f729e25` | 2026-04-28 | 2026-06-02 | `edgesynology2` | warning | waiting_on_user | Sustained disk I/O pressure |
+| `23f69e28` | 2026-04-28 | 2026-05-07 | `edgesynology1` | warning | waiting_on_user | Sustained disk I/O pressure |
+| `13195cd4` | 2026-04-30 | 2026-06-03 | `edgesynology1` | warning | waiting_on_user | Repeated backup failures |
+| `cfa77036` | 2026-04-30 | 2026-05-07 | `edgesynology2` | critical | waiting_on_user | Repeated backup failures |
+| `b51a6982` | 2026-04-30 | 2026-04-30 | `edgesynology2` | warning | waiting_on_user | General sync conflicts |
+| `7911b42a` | 2026-05-01 | 2026-05-07 | both | critical | waiting_on_user | Synology Drive not-ready state |
+| `ff3e3c45` | 2026-05-01 | 2026-05-07 | both | critical | waiting_on_user | ShareSync metadata lookup failures |
+| `1533ac69` | 2026-05-01 | 2026-05-07 | `edgesynology1` | critical | waiting_on_user | Recurring sync failures in `admin_console` |
+| `b5df26e9` | 2026-05-07 | 2026-06-03 | `edgesynology1` | critical | waiting_for_approval | Recurring sync failures in Drive |
+| `32689255` | 2026-05-31 | 2026-06-03 | `edgesynology1` | critical | waiting_on_user | Hyper Backup `task_3` failures |
+| `94586fe2` | 2026-05-31 | 2026-06-03 | `edgesynology2` | critical | waiting_on_user | Recurring ShareSync transport issue |
+| `0053b130` | 2026-06-02 | 2026-06-03 | `edgesynology1` | critical | running | Drive permission and conflict-loop issue |
+| `2b01f412` | 2026-06-02 | 2026-06-02 | `edgesynology1` | critical | waiting_on_user | Drive NativeUpload backup failures |
+| `40be5ebf` | 2026-06-02 | 2026-06-03 | `edgesynology1` | warning | waiting_on_user | Rename sync conflicts and `/Decor/Generic` queue jam |
+| `e100998c` | 2026-06-03 | 2026-06-03 | `edgesynology2` | warning | open | Rename sync conflicts |
+
+### `babfc04f` - ShareSync API failures in `admin_console`
+
+The backend grouped 670 Drive admin and ShareSync API failures across both NAS units. The monitor classified this as a broken ShareSync control-plane problem rather than isolated user-file errors. The strongest diagnostic evidence pointed at `edgesynology1`: `/volume1` was absent, `/dev/mapper/vg1-*` and `/dev/vg1` were absent, the `lvm` binary was missing, `synopkg` was missing, `/var/packages/SynologyDrive` was missing, and `/var/log/messages` was missing. At the same time, the root filesystem was still partially alive on `/dev/mapper/cachedev_0`, and RAID arrays `md0` through `md9` were structurally intact with healthy member states.
+
+The working diagnosis was destruction or severe breakage of the DSM volume/package layer above an intact block layer. That explains why Drive Server could not register the ShareSync API modules and why calls to `SYNO.SynologyDrive.ShareSync` and `SYNO.SynologyDrive.Admin.ShareSync` returned invalid WebAPI responses. The backend also recorded that backups were failing on both units by April 14, 2026. This issue is important because it may be an upstream or background cause for later Drive and ShareSync symptoms.
+
+### `f5ef174d` - Mass rename activity on `edgesynology1`
+
+The monitor grouped 248 rename alerts into a single critical issue. Alert evidence included ransomware-class detections such as 108 file events in 60 seconds under paths including `SharedLic/Warner Bros/Renewals/2027 Renewal` and `SharedProd/Shipments/2026 - 04 [Apr]/FOB DOLLAR GENERAL D3387`. Some representative file names included temporary Office-style names such as `7084A00.tmp` and normal business files such as filtered sales-order spreadsheets and shipment ZIP files.
+
+This issue should not be treated as proven ransomware by itself. The backend label is based on rename volume and velocity. It is still a unique problem because the rename storm can poison sync state, create conflict copies, trigger backup file-level errors, and create downstream ShareSync queue pressure. Later `task_3` backup failures were explicitly suspected to involve problematic files left behind by a related mass-rename event in the `SharedProd/Shipments` tree.
+
+### `7f729e25` - Sustained disk I/O pressure on `edgesynology2`
+
+The backend grouped 107 high-I/O-wait readings on `edgesynology2`, with average CPU iowait around 29.0% and peak iowait around 46.8%. Raw fact aggregation in the same two-month window showed hundreds of `io_wait_high` facts for `edgesynology2`, including both warning and critical readings.
+
+This is a unique infrastructure problem because high disk wait blocks ordinary DSM work, slows Drive and ShareSync worker processes, and can make API registration or socket-based service calls appear intermittently broken. It also overlaps with later evidence from `edgesynology2` showing RAID resync or scrub activity, saturated disk queues, and ShareSync transport instability.
+
+### `23f69e28` - Sustained disk I/O pressure on `edgesynology1`
+
+The backend grouped 40 high-I/O-wait readings on `edgesynology1`, with average CPU iowait around 21.2% and peak iowait around 32.7%. Raw facts also showed warning and critical `io_wait_high` observations for `edgesynology1` during April and early May.
+
+This issue matters because it can amplify otherwise small Drive problems. When the disk layer is slow, Drive upload workers and ShareSync queue processors can time out or retry. That can make metadata churn, large binary uploads, and backup jobs fail in ways that look like higher-level application bugs.
+
+### `13195cd4` - Repeated backup failures on `edgesynology1`
+
+The monitor grouped 9 backup failures on `edgesynology1` under the generic `backup` detector. The stored issue record did not contain a complete final root-cause narrative, but it remained open through June 3 and was repeatedly escalated as requiring deeper investigation.
+
+This should be tracked separately from the later `task_3` and Drive NativeUpload backup failures because it was first detected earlier, on April 30, and used a different fingerprint. It may represent a broader backup subsystem failure, a source-file readability problem, or another symptom of the same volume/package-layer instability recorded in the April 12 ShareSync API issue.
+
+### `cfa77036` - Repeated backup failures on `edgesynology2`
+
+The monitor grouped 2 backup failures on `edgesynology2` under the generic `backup` detector. It was marked critical and remained waiting on user input. Like the matching `edgesynology1` backup thread, the backend record did not retain a complete final root-cause narrative in the issue summary.
+
+This still counts as a unique problem because it affected the other NAS and was independently detected. It also lines up with the broader pattern that backups were reported failing on both units by April 14 and that `edgesynology2` later showed heavy I/O pressure and stalled Hyper Backup integrity work.
+
+### `b51a6982` - General sync conflicts on `edgesynology2`
+
+The backend grouped 2 general sync-conflict events on `edgesynology2`. The detector did not assign a more specific file path or subsystem beyond the general sync-conflict class.
+
+This is a smaller signal than the later rename-specific conflict issues, but it belongs in the ledger because it shows that `edgesynology2` was already generating conflict-level sync telemetry by April 30. It may be an early surface symptom of the same ShareSync control-plane and I/O-pressure problems.
+
+### `7911b42a` - Synology Drive not-ready state across both units
+
+The monitor grouped 31 Drive API calls where Cloud Station or Synology Drive reported a not-ready state. The issue affected both `edgesynology1` and `edgesynology2` and was marked critical.
+
+This is likely part of the same control-plane family as the ShareSync API-invalid issue. A not-ready Drive state can occur when Drive Server is not initialized, is unable to access its package/database state, is blocked by system resource pressure, or cannot register expected APIs. In this incident window, the backend evidence already included missing Drive package directories on `edgesynology1` and later ShareSync metadata lookup failures across both NAS units.
+
+### `ff3e3c45` - ShareSync metadata lookup failures
+
+The backend grouped 4 Drive error events where ShareSync failed to read or parse shared-folder metadata. The issue affected both units and was marked critical. The detector described this as a backend ShareSync fault rather than a front-end warning.
+
+This is important because ShareSync depends on correct shared-folder metadata and internal Drive state to map folders, tasks, and remote endpoints. Metadata lookup failures can make ShareSync behave as if folders or tasks are absent, invalid, or unready even when files still exist on disk. This problem connects directly to the visible symptoms of missing/inaccessible files and to the repeated invalid-state API failures.
+
+### `1533ac69` - Recurring sync failures in `admin_console`
+
+The backend grouped 6 sync failure events in `admin_console` on `edgesynology1`. It was marked critical and associated with the same Drive or ShareSync fault pattern as other early-May control-plane issues.
+
+This issue is distinct because it was detected through admin-console sync-failure telemetry rather than API-invalid or metadata-lookup telemetry. It is another view of the same operational breakdown: the management plane could see that sync work was failing, but the underlying cause likely lived in Drive/ShareSync state, package registration, missing metadata, or the damaged DSM volume/package layer.
+
+### `b5df26e9` - Recurring sync failures in Drive / stalled Hyper Backup integrity work
+
+The issue title says recurring sync failures in Drive, but the stored hypothesis points to a specific resource-contention failure: a Hyper Backup integrity check on `edgesynology2` was stuck for roughly 18 hours. The backend identified `synoimgbkptool` with more than 16 hours of CPU time and D-state uninterruptible I/O sleep. It also recorded saturated I/O queues on `md5`, `dm-1`, `dm-2`, and `sdd6`, apparently caused by a concurrent RAID data scrub on `edgesynology2`.
+
+The working interpretation was that the backup was alive but indefinitely stalled, not crashed. This is a unique failure mode: it is not primarily a file conflict or API registration problem, but a resource contention deadlock between backup verification and storage maintenance. It also helps explain why ShareSync and Drive could become unstable on `edgesynology2` during the same period.
+
+### `32689255` - Hyper Backup `task_3` failures on `edgesynology1`
+
+The monitor grouped 4 backup failures for `task_3` on `edgesynology1`. The stored hypothesis was that Hyper Backup was failing with `ErrorDetect` on every run because one or more source files were problematic or unreadable. The most likely area was the `SharedProd/Shipments/2026-04 Apr/FOB DOLLAR GENERAL D3387` directory.
+
+The backend connected this to mass file rename activity: a drive churn signal with `rename=185`, `delete=185`, and `conflict=2` occurred about 30 seconds before the first `task_3` failure. The evidence did not point to hardware, network, or general I/O as the primary cause for this specific task. The open question is which exact file-level error inside the Hyper Backup task log names the failing source file.
+
+### `94586fe2` - ShareSync transport instability on `edgesynology2`
+
+The backend grouped 250 related telemetry events for ShareSync on `edgesynology2`. The stored diagnosis split the problem into two windows. On May 29, 2026 from about 22:44 to 22:54 UTC, connection 3 had 8 disconnects but lacked enough correlated telemetry to prove the trigger. On May 31, 2026 from about 23:40 to 23:43 UTC, a RAID5 resync on `md5` was around 65.7% to 66.0% complete and drove high disk utilization and latency.
+
+During the May 31 window, `secure_login_logger` socket failures occurred across 77 ShareSync worker processes. The backend interpreted this as a clean chain: RAID resync I/O saturation caused secure-login socket pressure, which caused ShareSync authentication/transport drops. A 25-day telemetry gap from May 6 to May 31 means the resync start and May 29 trigger are still unconfirmed. The sync backlog showed move/delete/conflict/reconnect patterns that may be residual from the May 6 mass-rename event.
+
+### `0053b130` - Drive permission/membership problem plus large-file conflict loop
+
+The backend grouped 5 Drive telemetry events on `edgesynology1`, but the stored hypothesis separated them into two independent problems. First, user `IML\eperestrelo` was attempting to access the `styleguides` Team Folder at ViewId 18 under `/volume1/styleguides/Disney/Mickey/`, while their Drive user record was bound to ViewId 51. The backend described all-capability-zero ACL denials for nodes under `/Disney/Mickey/`, consistent with a Drive-layer membership gap rather than a proven filesystem ACL issue.
+
+Second, a large TIF file under `/volume1/mac/Decor/Generic Decor/_New structure/AA1/16x16/AA166MSFSH06/ART/` had spawned 7 zero-byte conflict copies from multiple client machines. Drive NativeUpload workers repeatedly failed reverse-delta calculation, fell back to whole-file upload, failed to prepare materials, and retried. That conflict loop was also tied to the `/Decor/Generic` ShareSync queue jam with repeat counts in the 136 to 194 range per 5-minute window.
+
+### `2b01f412` - Drive NativeUpload backup failures on `edgesynology1`
+
+The backend grouped 2 Drive NativeUpload backup failures on `edgesynology1`. The stored hypothesis identified two concurrent causes. One was a large PST file at `/volume1/mgmt/ahazan/Computer/PST backup 2025-10-16 3-48pm.pst` that appeared to be actively written or locked by an Outlook-like client, with a companion `.tmp` file generating file-not-modified/fake-event warnings. The Drive worker could not get a stable read of the PST and aborted upload preparation.
+
+The second cause was a large TIF file in `/volume1/mac/Decor/Generic Decor/...AA166MSFSH06_16x16x1_Printed Canvas w Foil_Chanel Perfume Bottle Gold Heart_ART.tif` stuck behind the same `/Decor/Generic` ShareSync queue jam. Multiple Drive workers attempted reverse-delta and whole-file fallback and failed. The backend also recorded `invoked store.cpp:229 failed to parse response` errors between 18:50 and 19:23 UTC, suggesting the Drive task invocation layer was failing while the queue was overloaded.
+
+### `40be5ebf` - Rename sync conflicts and `/Decor/Generic` queue jam on `edgesynology1`
+
+The backend grouped 37 rename sync-conflict events on `edgesynology1`. The stored diagnosis attributed the events to heavy macOS client churn against `/Decor/Generic` and the adjacent `/wait for designer approve/_GENERIC/*` subtree. Samples showed roughly 275 macOS-related filesystem hits per collection interval, with repeated rename, delete, and move activity.
+
+The monitor interpreted the sync-conflict label as a surface symptom rather than real user-to-user conflict. The likely mechanism was a write-amplification queue jam: the path was requeued 130 to 194 times per sampling window, and each new rename invalidated in-flight sync work before a `DoneEvent` could clear the queue. Drive also logged file-not-modified/fake-event warnings, indicating that many events changed metadata or filenames rather than file contents.
+
+### `e100998c` - Rename sync conflicts on `edgesynology2`
+
+The backend grouped 39 rename sync-conflict events on `edgesynology2` on June 3, 2026. The available issue details were thinner than the matching `edgesynology1` thread, but the seed events showed a recurring Drive churn signal with `rename=1`, `delete=62`, `move=38`, and `conflict=0`, with `mac_hits=0`.
+
+This is a unique `edgesynology2` problem because it involved a different NAS and a different churn profile than the `/Decor/Generic` macOS-heavy queue jam on `edgesynology1`. It should be investigated as either a downstream replication effect, a server-side batch delete/move workflow, or a separate ShareSync queue issue.
+
+### Raw backend signals supporting the ledger
+
+The raw `facts` and `alerts` tables show why the grouped issue list should not be read as isolated single events:
+
+- `edgesynology2` had 491 warning and 345 critical `CPU is blocked on disk I/O` facts between April 8 and May 7.
+- `edgesynology1` had 133 warning and 7 critical `CPU is blocked on disk I/O` facts between April 8 and May 1.
+- `edgesynology1` generated 750 critical ransomware-class mass-file-rename alerts between April 7 and May 6.
+- `edgesynology2` generated 248 critical ShareSync queue-jam alerts between June 1 and June 2.
+- `edgesynology2` generated ShareSync transport-instability alerts on May 29.
+- `edgesynology2` had single critical log anomalies for `Database access failure` and `Massive secure_login_logger failure` on May 1.
+- `edgesynology1` had a warning log pattern for recurring Snapshot Replication API unavailability on April 16.
+
+These raw signals reinforce the working conclusion that the environment had multiple overlapping failure modes: storage I/O saturation, Drive/ShareSync control-plane damage, queue jams from rename/delete churn, backup file-level failures, and local permission or metadata breakage.
 
 ## Incident Chronology
 
 This chronology only includes dates that can be supported by snapshots, MCP metadata, or existing reports.
*** End Patch

### 5. Broader Decor discrepancies exist outside the single-folder example

A separate local comparison report for `Generic Decor.old` vs current `Generic Decor` found:

- 70 files missing by same relative path
- 45 directories missing by same relative path
- those 70 files were not found elsewhere in the base tree by content hash

So the total problem set is broader than one `PPS Photos` folder.

## Backend Problem Ledger

The following ledger was pulled from the live Synology Monitor backend on June 5, 2026. It covers unique grouped `issues` updated between April 5, 2026 and June 5, 2026. Raw `alerts` and `facts` in the same window were also checked; they mainly support the same grouped issue families: mass rename/ransomware-class alerts, disk I/O pressure, ShareSync queue jams, and secure-login/socket failures.

As of the query time, the backend had 17 unique issue threads in this two-month window. None were marked resolved.

### `babfc04f` - ShareSync API failures in `admin_console`

The backend grouped 670 Drive admin and ShareSync API failures across both NAS units. The monitor classified this as a broken ShareSync control-plane problem rather than isolated user-file errors. The strongest diagnostic evidence pointed at `edgesynology1`: `/volume1` was absent, `/dev/mapper/vg1-*` and `/dev/vg1` were absent, the `lvm` binary was missing, `synopkg` was missing, `/var/packages/SynologyDrive` was missing, and `/var/log/messages` was missing. At the same time, the root filesystem was still partially alive on `/dev/mapper/cachedev_0`, and RAID arrays `md0` through `md9` were structurally intact with healthy member states.

The working diagnosis was destruction or severe breakage of the DSM volume/package layer above an intact block layer. That explains why Drive Server could not register the ShareSync API modules and why calls to `SYNO.SynologyDrive.ShareSync` and `SYNO.SynologyDrive.Admin.ShareSync` returned invalid WebAPI responses. The backend also recorded that backups were failing on both units by April 14, 2026. This issue is important because it may be an upstream or background cause for later Drive and ShareSync symptoms.

### `f5ef174d` - Mass rename activity on `edgesynology1`

The monitor grouped 248 rename alerts into a single critical issue. Alert evidence included ransomware-class detections such as 108 file events in 60 seconds under paths including `SharedLic/Warner Bros/Renewals/2027 Renewal` and `SharedProd/Shipments/2026 - 04 [Apr]/FOB DOLLAR GENERAL D3387`. Some representative file names included temporary Office-style names such as `7084A00.tmp` and normal business files such as filtered sales-order spreadsheets and shipment ZIP files.

This issue should not be treated as proven ransomware by itself. The backend label is based on rename volume and velocity. It is still a unique problem because the rename storm can poison sync state, create conflict copies, trigger backup file-level errors, and create downstream ShareSync queue pressure. Later `task_3` backup failures were explicitly suspected to involve problematic files left behind by a related mass-rename event in the `SharedProd/Shipments` tree.

### `7f729e25` - Sustained disk I/O pressure on `edgesynology2`

The backend grouped 107 high-I/O-wait readings on `edgesynology2`, with average CPU iowait around 29.0% and peak iowait around 46.8%. Raw fact aggregation in the same two-month window showed hundreds of `io_wait_high` facts for `edgesynology2`, including both warning and critical readings.

This is a unique infrastructure problem because high disk wait blocks ordinary DSM work, slows Drive and ShareSync worker processes, and can make API registration or socket-based service calls appear intermittently broken. It also overlaps with later evidence from `edgesynology2` showing RAID resync or scrub activity, saturated disk queues, and ShareSync transport instability.

### `23f69e28` - Sustained disk I/O pressure on `edgesynology1`

The backend grouped 40 high-I/O-wait readings on `edgesynology1`, with average CPU iowait around 21.2% and peak iowait around 32.7%. Raw facts also showed warning and critical `io_wait_high` observations for `edgesynology1` during April and early May.

This issue matters because it can amplify otherwise small Drive problems. When the disk layer is slow, Drive upload workers and ShareSync queue processors can time out or retry. That can make metadata churn, large binary uploads, and backup jobs fail in ways that look like higher-level application bugs.

### `13195cd4` - Repeated backup failures on `edgesynology1`

The monitor grouped 9 backup failures on `edgesynology1` under the generic `backup` detector. The stored issue record did not contain a complete final root-cause narrative, but it remained open through June 3 and was repeatedly escalated as requiring deeper investigation.

This should be tracked separately from the later `task_3` and Drive NativeUpload backup failures because it was first detected earlier, on April 30, and used a different fingerprint. It may represent a broader backup subsystem failure, a source-file readability problem, or another symptom of the same volume/package-layer instability recorded in the April 12 ShareSync API issue.

### `cfa77036` - Repeated backup failures on `edgesynology2`

The monitor grouped 2 backup failures on `edgesynology2` under the generic `backup` detector. It was marked critical and remained waiting on user input. Like the matching `edgesynology1` backup thread, the backend record did not retain a complete final root-cause narrative in the issue summary.

This still counts as a unique problem because it affected the other NAS and was independently detected. It also lines up with the broader pattern that backups were reported failing on both units by April 14 and that `edgesynology2` later showed heavy I/O pressure and stalled Hyper Backup integrity work.

### `b51a6982` - General sync conflicts on `edgesynology2`

The backend grouped 2 general sync-conflict events on `edgesynology2`. The detector did not assign a more specific file path or subsystem beyond the general sync-conflict class.

This is a smaller signal than the later rename-specific conflict issues, but it belongs in the ledger because it shows that `edgesynology2` was already generating conflict-level sync telemetry by April 30. It may be an early surface symptom of the same ShareSync control-plane and I/O-pressure problems.

### `7911b42a` - Synology Drive not-ready state across both units

The monitor grouped 31 Drive API calls where Cloud Station or Synology Drive reported a not-ready state. The issue affected both `edgesynology1` and `edgesynology2` and was marked critical.

This is likely part of the same control-plane family as the ShareSync API-invalid issue. A not-ready Drive state can occur when Drive Server is not initialized, is unable to access its package/database state, is blocked by system resource pressure, or cannot register expected APIs. In this incident window, the backend evidence already included missing Drive package directories on `edgesynology1` and later ShareSync metadata lookup failures across both NAS units.

### `ff3e3c45` - ShareSync metadata lookup failures

The backend grouped 4 Drive error events where ShareSync failed to read or parse shared-folder metadata. The issue affected both units and was marked critical. The detector described this as a backend ShareSync fault rather than a front-end warning.

This is important because ShareSync depends on correct shared-folder metadata and internal Drive state to map folders, tasks, and remote endpoints. Metadata lookup failures can make ShareSync behave as if folders or tasks are absent, invalid, or unready even when files still exist on disk. This problem connects directly to the visible symptoms of missing/inaccessible files and to the repeated invalid-state API failures.

### `1533ac69` - Recurring sync failures in `admin_console`

The backend grouped 6 sync failure events in `admin_console` on `edgesynology1`. It was marked critical and associated with the same Drive or ShareSync fault pattern as other early-May control-plane issues.

This issue is distinct because it was detected through admin-console sync-failure telemetry rather than API-invalid or metadata-lookup telemetry. It is another view of the same operational breakdown: the management plane could see that sync work was failing, but the underlying cause likely lived in Drive/ShareSync state, package registration, missing metadata, or the damaged DSM volume/package layer.

### `b5df26e9` - Recurring sync failures in Drive / stalled Hyper Backup integrity work

The issue title says recurring sync failures in Drive, but the stored hypothesis points to a specific resource-contention failure: a Hyper Backup integrity check on `edgesynology2` was stuck for roughly 18 hours. The backend identified `synoimgbkptool` with more than 16 hours of CPU time and D-state uninterruptible I/O sleep. It also recorded saturated I/O queues on `md5`, `dm-1`, `dm-2`, and `sdd6`, apparently caused by a concurrent RAID data scrub on `edgesynology2`.

The working interpretation was that the backup was alive but indefinitely stalled, not crashed. This is a unique failure mode: it is not primarily a file conflict or API registration problem, but a resource contention deadlock between backup verification and storage maintenance. It also helps explain why ShareSync and Drive could become unstable on `edgesynology2` during the same period.

### `32689255` - Hyper Backup `task_3` failures on `edgesynology1`

The monitor grouped 4 backup failures for `task_3` on `edgesynology1`. The stored hypothesis was that Hyper Backup was failing with `ErrorDetect` on every run because one or more source files were problematic or unreadable. The most likely area was the `SharedProd/Shipments/2026-04 Apr/FOB DOLLAR GENERAL D3387` directory.

The backend connected this to mass file rename activity: a drive churn signal with `rename=185`, `delete=185`, and `conflict=2` occurred about 30 seconds before the first `task_3` failure. The evidence did not point to hardware, network, or general I/O as the primary cause for this specific task. The open question is which exact file-level error inside the Hyper Backup task log names the failing source file.

### `94586fe2` - ShareSync transport instability on `edgesynology2`

The backend grouped 250 related telemetry events for ShareSync on `edgesynology2`. The stored diagnosis split the problem into two windows. On May 29, 2026 from about 22:44 to 22:54 UTC, connection 3 had 8 disconnects but lacked enough correlated telemetry to prove the trigger. On May 31, 2026 from about 23:40 to 23:43 UTC, a RAID5 resync on `md5` was around 65.7% to 66.0% complete and drove high disk utilization and latency.

During the May 31 window, `secure_login_logger` socket failures occurred across 77 ShareSync worker processes. The backend interpreted this as a clean chain: RAID resync I/O saturation caused secure-login socket pressure, which caused ShareSync authentication/transport drops. A 25-day telemetry gap from May 6 to May 31 means the resync start and May 29 trigger are still unconfirmed. The sync backlog showed move/delete/conflict/reconnect patterns that may be residual from the May 6 mass-rename event.

### `0053b130` - Drive permission/membership problem plus large-file conflict loop

The backend grouped 5 Drive telemetry events on `edgesynology1`, but the stored hypothesis separated them into two independent problems. First, user `IML\eperestrelo` was attempting to access the `styleguides` Team Folder at ViewId 18 under `/volume1/styleguides/Disney/Mickey/`, while their Drive user record was bound to ViewId 51. The backend described all-capability-zero ACL denials for nodes under `/Disney/Mickey/`, consistent with a Drive-layer membership gap rather than a proven filesystem ACL issue.

Second, a large TIF file under `/volume1/mac/Decor/Generic Decor/_New structure/AA1/16x16/AA166MSFSH06/ART/` had spawned 7 zero-byte conflict copies from multiple client machines. Drive NativeUpload workers repeatedly failed reverse-delta calculation, fell back to whole-file upload, failed to prepare materials, and retried. That conflict loop was also tied to the `/Decor/Generic` ShareSync queue jam with repeat counts in the 136 to 194 range per 5-minute window.

### `2b01f412` - Drive NativeUpload backup failures on `edgesynology1`

The backend grouped 2 Drive NativeUpload backup failures on `edgesynology1`. The stored hypothesis identified two concurrent causes. One was a large PST file at `/volume1/mgmt/ahazan/Computer/PST backup 2025-10-16 3-48pm.pst` that appeared to be actively written or locked by an Outlook-like client, with a companion `.tmp` file generating file-not-modified/fake-event warnings. The Drive worker could not get a stable read of the PST and aborted upload preparation.

The second cause was a large TIF file in `/volume1/mac/Decor/Generic Decor/...AA166MSFSH06_16x16x1_Printed Canvas w Foil_Chanel Perfume Bottle Gold Heart_ART.tif` stuck behind the same `/Decor/Generic` ShareSync queue jam. Multiple Drive workers attempted reverse-delta and whole-file fallback and failed. The backend also recorded `invoked store.cpp:229 failed to parse response` errors between 18:50 and 19:23 UTC, suggesting the Drive task invocation layer was failing while the queue was overloaded.

### `40be5ebf` - Rename sync conflicts and `/Decor/Generic` queue jam on `edgesynology1`

The backend grouped 37 rename sync-conflict events on `edgesynology1`. The stored diagnosis attributed the events to heavy macOS client churn against `/Decor/Generic` and the adjacent `/wait for designer approve/_GENERIC/*` subtree. Samples showed roughly 275 macOS-related filesystem hits per collection interval, with repeated rename, delete, and move activity.

The monitor interpreted the sync-conflict label as a surface symptom rather than real user-to-user conflict. The likely mechanism was a write-amplification queue jam: the path was requeued 130 to 194 times per sampling window, and each new rename invalidated in-flight sync work before a `DoneEvent` could clear the queue. Drive also logged file-not-modified/fake-event warnings, indicating that many events changed metadata or filenames rather than file contents.

### `e100998c` - Rename sync conflicts on `edgesynology2`

The backend grouped 39 rename sync-conflict events on `edgesynology2` on June 3, 2026. The available issue details were thinner than the matching `edgesynology1` thread, but the seed events showed a recurring Drive churn signal with `rename=1`, `delete=62`, `move=38`, and `conflict=0`, with `mac_hits=0`.

This is a unique `edgesynology2` problem because it involved a different NAS and a different churn profile than the `/Decor/Generic` macOS-heavy queue jam on `edgesynology1`. It should be investigated as either a downstream replication effect, a server-side batch delete/move workflow, or a separate ShareSync queue issue.

### Raw backend signals supporting the ledger

The raw `facts` and `alerts` tables show why the grouped issue list should not be read as isolated single events:

- `edgesynology2` had 491 warning and 345 critical `CPU is blocked on disk I/O` facts between April 8 and May 7.
- `edgesynology1` had 133 warning and 7 critical `CPU is blocked on disk I/O` facts between April 8 and May 1.
- `edgesynology1` generated 750 critical ransomware-class mass-file-rename alerts between April 7 and May 6.
- `edgesynology2` generated 248 critical ShareSync queue-jam alerts between June 1 and June 2.
- `edgesynology2` generated ShareSync transport-instability alerts on May 29.
- `edgesynology2` had single critical log anomalies for `Database access failure` and `Massive secure_login_logger failure` on May 1.
- `edgesynology1` had a warning log pattern for recurring Snapshot Replication API unavailability on April 16.

These raw signals reinforce the working conclusion that the environment had multiple overlapping failure modes: storage I/O saturation, Drive/ShareSync control-plane damage, queue jams from rename/delete churn, backup file-level failures, and local permission or metadata breakage.

+The monitor grouped 2 backup failures on `edgesynology2` under the generic `backup` detector. It was marked critical and remained waiting on user input. Like the matching `edgesynology1` backup thread, the backend record did not retain a complete final root-cause narrative in the issue summary.
+
+This still counts as a unique problem because it affected the other NAS and was independently detected. It also lines up with the broader pattern that backups were reported failing on both units by April 14 and that `edgesynology2` later showed heavy I/O pressure and stalled Hyper Backup integrity work.
+
+### `b51a6982` - General sync conflicts on `edgesynology2`
+
+The backend grouped 2 general sync-conflict events on `edgesynology2`. The detector did not assign a more specific file path or subsystem beyond the general sync-conflict class.
+
+This is a smaller signal than the later rename-specific conflict issues, but it belongs in the ledger because it shows that `edgesynology2` was already generating conflict-level sync telemetry by April 30. It may be an early surface symptom of the same ShareSync control-plane and I/O-pressure problems.
+
+### `7911b42a` - Synology Drive not-ready state across both units
+
+The monitor grouped 31 Drive API calls where Cloud Station or Synology Drive reported a not-ready state. The issue affected both `edgesynology1` and `edgesynology2` and was marked critical.
+
+This is likely part of the same control-plane family as the ShareSync API-invalid issue. A not-ready Drive state can occur when Drive Server is not initialized, is unable to access its package/database state, is blocked by system resource pressure, or cannot register expected APIs. In this incident window, the backend evidence already included missing Drive package directories on `edgesynology1` and later ShareSync metadata lookup failures across both NAS units.
+
+### `ff3e3c45` - ShareSync metadata lookup failures
+
+The backend grouped 4 Drive error events where ShareSync failed to read or parse shared-folder metadata. The issue affected both units and was marked critical. The detector described this as a backend ShareSync fault rather than a front-end warning.
+
+This is important because ShareSync depends on correct shared-folder metadata and internal Drive state to map folders, tasks, and remote endpoints. Metadata lookup failures can make ShareSync behave as if folders or tasks are absent, invalid, or unready even when files still exist on disk. This problem connects directly to the visible symptoms of missing/inaccessible files and to the repeated invalid-state API failures.
+
+### `1533ac69` - Recurring sync failures in `admin_console`
+
+The backend grouped 6 sync failure events in `admin_console` on `edgesynology1`. It was marked critical and associated with the same Drive or ShareSync fault pattern as other early-May control-plane issues.
+
+This issue is distinct because it was detected through admin-console sync-failure telemetry rather than API-invalid or metadata-lookup telemetry. It is another view of the same operational breakdown: the management plane could see that sync work was failing, but the underlying cause likely lived in Drive/ShareSync state, package registration, missing metadata, or the damaged DSM volume/package layer.
+
+### `b5df26e9` - Recurring sync failures in Drive / stalled Hyper Backup integrity work
+
+The issue title says recurring sync failures in Drive, but the stored hypothesis points to a specific resource-contention failure: a Hyper Backup integrity check on `edgesynology2` was stuck for roughly 18 hours. The backend identified `synoimgbkptool` with more than 16 hours of CPU time and D-state uninterruptible I/O sleep. It also recorded saturated I/O queues on `md5`, `dm-1`, `dm-2`, and `sdd6`, apparently caused by a concurrent RAID data scrub on `edgesynology2`.
+
+The working interpretation was that the backup was alive but indefinitely stalled, not crashed. This is a unique failure mode: it is not primarily a file conflict or API registration problem, but a resource contention deadlock between backup verification and storage maintenance. It also helps explain why ShareSync and Drive could become unstable on `edgesynology2` during the same period.
+
+### `32689255` - Hyper Backup `task_3` failures on `edgesynology1`
+
+The monitor grouped 4 backup failures for `task_3` on `edgesynology1`. The stored hypothesis was that Hyper Backup was failing with `ErrorDetect` on every run because one or more source files were problematic or unreadable. The most likely area was the `SharedProd/Shipments/2026-04 Apr/FOB DOLLAR GENERAL D3387` directory.
+
+The backend connected this to mass file rename activity: a drive churn signal with `rename=185`, `delete=185`, and `conflict=2` occurred about 30 seconds before the first `task_3` failure. The evidence did not point to hardware, network, or general I/O as the primary cause for this specific task. The open question is which exact file-level error inside the Hyper Backup task log names the failing source file.
+
+### `94586fe2` - ShareSync transport instability on `edgesynology2`
+
+The backend grouped 250 related telemetry events for ShareSync on `edgesynology2`. The stored diagnosis split the problem into two windows. On May 29, 2026 from about 22:44 to 22:54 UTC, connection 3 had 8 disconnects but lacked enough correlated telemetry to prove the trigger. On May 31, 2026 from about 23:40 to 23:43 UTC, a RAID5 resync on `md5` was around 65.7% to 66.0% complete and drove high disk utilization and latency.
+
+During the May 31 window, `secure_login_logger` socket failures occurred across 77 ShareSync worker processes. The backend interpreted this as a clean chain: RAID resync I/O saturation caused secure-login socket pressure, which caused ShareSync authentication/transport drops. A 25-day telemetry gap from May 6 to May 31 means the resync start and May 29 trigger are still unconfirmed. The sync backlog showed move/delete/conflict/reconnect patterns that may be residual from the May 6 mass-rename event.
+
+### `0053b130` - Drive permission/membership problem plus large-file conflict loop
+
+The backend grouped 5 Drive telemetry events on `edgesynology1`, but the stored hypothesis separated them into two independent problems. First, user `IML\eperestrelo` was attempting to access the `styleguides` Team Folder at ViewId 18 under `/volume1/styleguides/Disney/Mickey/`, while their Drive user record was bound to ViewId 51. The backend described all-capability-zero ACL denials for nodes under `/Disney/Mickey/`, consistent with a Drive-layer membership gap rather than a proven filesystem ACL issue.
+
+Second, a large TIF file under `/volume1/mac/Decor/Generic Decor/_New structure/AA1/16x16/AA166MSFSH06/ART/` had spawned 7 zero-byte conflict copies from multiple client machines. Drive NativeUpload workers repeatedly failed reverse-delta calculation, fell back to whole-file upload, failed to prepare materials, and retried. That conflict loop was also tied to the `/Decor/Generic` ShareSync queue jam with repeat counts in the 136 to 194 range per 5-minute window.
+
+### `2b01f412` - Drive NativeUpload backup failures on `edgesynology1`
+
+The backend grouped 2 Drive NativeUpload backup failures on `edgesynology1`. The stored hypothesis identified two concurrent causes. One was a large PST file at `/volume1/mgmt/ahazan/Computer/PST backup 2025-10-16 3-48pm.pst` that appeared to be actively written or locked by an Outlook-like client, with a companion `.tmp` file generating file-not-modified/fake-event warnings. The Drive worker could not get a stable read of the PST and aborted upload preparation.
+
+The second cause was a large TIF file in `/volume1/mac/Decor/Generic Decor/...AA166MSFSH06_16x16x1_Printed Canvas w Foil_Chanel Perfume Bottle Gold Heart_ART.tif` stuck behind the same `/Decor/Generic` ShareSync queue jam. Multiple Drive workers attempted reverse-delta and whole-file fallback and failed. The backend also recorded `invoked store.cpp:229 failed to parse response` errors between 18:50 and 19:23 UTC, suggesting the Drive task invocation layer was failing while the queue was overloaded.
+
+### `40be5ebf` - Rename sync conflicts and `/Decor/Generic` queue jam on `edgesynology1`
+
+The backend grouped 37 rename sync-conflict events on `edgesynology1`. The stored diagnosis attributed the events to heavy macOS client churn against `/Decor/Generic` and the adjacent `/wait for designer approve/_GENERIC/*` subtree. Samples showed roughly 275 macOS-related filesystem hits per collection interval, with repeated rename, delete, and move activity.
+
+The monitor interpreted the sync-conflict label as a surface symptom rather than real user-to-user conflict. The likely mechanism was a write-amplification queue jam: the path was requeued 130 to 194 times per sampling window, and each new rename invalidated in-flight sync work before a `DoneEvent` could clear the queue. Drive also logged file-not-modified/fake-event warnings, indicating that many events changed metadata or filenames rather than file contents.
+
+### `e100998c` - Rename sync conflicts on `edgesynology2`
+
+The backend grouped 39 rename sync-conflict events on `edgesynology2` on June 3, 2026. The available issue details were thinner than the matching `edgesynology1` thread, but the seed events showed a recurring Drive churn signal with `rename=1`, `delete=62`, `move=38`, and `conflict=0`, with `mac_hits=0`.
+
+This is a unique `edgesynology2` problem because it involved a different NAS and a different churn profile than the `/Decor/Generic` macOS-heavy queue jam on `edgesynology1`. It should be investigated as either a downstream replication effect, a server-side batch delete/move workflow, or a separate ShareSync queue issue.
+
+### Raw backend signals supporting the ledger
+
+The raw `facts` and `alerts` tables show why the grouped issue list should not be read as isolated single events:
+
+- `edgesynology2` had 491 warning and 345 critical `CPU is blocked on disk I/O` facts between April 8 and May 7.
+- `edgesynology1` had 133 warning and 7 critical `CPU is blocked on disk I/O` facts between April 8 and May 1.
+- `edgesynology1` generated 750 critical ransomware-class mass-file-rename alerts between April 7 and May 6.
+- `edgesynology2` generated 248 critical ShareSync queue-jam alerts between June 1 and June 2.
+- `edgesynology2` generated ShareSync transport-instability alerts on May 29.
+- `edgesynology2` had single critical log anomalies for `Database access failure` and `Massive secure_login_logger failure` on May 1.
+- `edgesynology1` had a warning log pattern for recurring Snapshot Replication API unavailability on April 16.
+
+These raw signals reinforce the working conclusion that the environment had multiple overlapping failure modes: storage I/O saturation, Drive/ShareSync control-plane damage, queue jams from rename/delete churn, backup file-level failures, and local permission or metadata breakage.
+
 ## Incident Chronology
 
 This chronology only includes dates that can be supported by snapshots, MCP metadata, or existing reports.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question.
